title: Incorrect handling of non-octal digits in octal string escape sequence
body:
I think there's also some extra mishandling going on with octal sequences that are less than 3 digits long.

Thanks for taking the time to report this.

Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, completely self-contained and self-checking so can directly run what you provide. Ideally use test_regress format, see https://veripool.org/guide/latest/contributing.html?highlight=test_regress#reporting-bugs

```systemverilog
module top;
	initial begin
		$display("\099 \119 \121"); // Q Q Q
		$display("\088 \108 \110"); // H H H
		$display("\102\3\12."); // B\023\312.
		$display("\102.\3.\12"); // B.\023.
	end
endmodule
```

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?
I used `verilator --binary hex.sv` and then ran the resulting executable, or inspecting the `VL_WRITEF_NX` output of `--cc`.

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
Tested with Verilator 5.033 devel rev v5.032-66-gcc1133c0d

What OS and distribution are you using?
Linux/Ubuntu 20.04

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
Sure
code_blocks:
module top;
	initial begin
		$display("\099 \119 \121"); // Q Q Q
		$display("\088 \108 \110"); // H H H
		$display("\102\3\12."); // B\023\312.
		$display("\102.\3.\12"); // B.\023.
	end
endmodule
